children.value: We approximated the posterior using neural posterior estimation (NPE) with learned summary statistics (
), as implemented in the BayesFlow 2.0 framework for amortized Bayesian inference (
Specificially, the implemented inference pipeline uses the following architecture:

Agent Simulation → Feature Extraction → DeepSet(64) → FlowMatching → Posterior
     (CPU)            (deterministic)     (summary)    (inference)   (4 params)

) maps the 40-dimensional feature vector (reshaped to a 1-element set) to a 64-dimensional summary representation. The DeepSet architecture is used (instead of an MLP) to comply with BayesFlow’s internal architecture requirements for condition passing.

children.html: h=DeepSet(x)∈R64\mathbf{h} = \text{DeepSet}(\mathbf{x}) \in \mathbb{R}^{64}h=DeepSet(x)∈R64

children.value: ) with optimal transport and an MLP subnet (4 layers × 256 units, 5% dropout) learns a conditional normalizing flow from the 64-dimensional summary to the 4-dimensional posterior over parameters. FlowMatching was chosen over coupling flows for its faster convergence and better handling of multimodal posteriors.

children.html: h\mathbf{h}h

children.value: conditions a Flow Matching model that learns a continuous normalizing flow from a standard normal base distribution to the conditional posterior:

children.html: θ=Fϕ(z;h),z∼N(0,I4)\boldsymbol{\theta} = F_\phi(\mathbf{z}; \mathbf{h}), \quad \mathbf{z} \sim \mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0}, \mathbf{I}_4)θ=Fϕ​(z;h),z∼N(0,I4​)
FϕF_\phiFϕ​

children.value: The pre-generated feature vectors are then used to train the DeepSet + FlowMatching network:
The network is trained offline (i.e., on the pre-generated dataset, not with on-the-fly simulation) for up to 300 epochs with:

children.html: 5×10−45 \times 10^{-4}5×10−4

children.value: Optimizer: Adam (this is the default Keras settings)
Early stopping: patience 50, restoring best weights (monitored on validation loss)
BayesFlow’s default training objective for Flow Matching is the conditional flow matching loss (
), which minimizes the expected squared error between the learned and target velocity fields:

children.html: L(ϕ)=Et,θ,z,x[∥vϕ(t,θt;h(x))−ut(θt∣θ)∥2]\mathcal{L}(\phi) = \mathbb{E}_{t, \boldsymbol{\theta}, \mathbf{z}, \mathbf{x}} \left[ \| v_\phi(t, \boldsymbol{\theta}_t; \mathbf{h}(\mathbf{x})) - u_t(\boldsymbol{\theta}_t \mid \boldsymbol{\theta}) \|^2 \right]L(ϕ)=Et,θ,z,x​[∥vϕ​(t,θt​;h(x))−ut​(θt​∣θ)∥2]
t∼Uniform(0,1)t \sim \text{Uniform}(0, 1)t∼Uniform(0,1)
θt=(1−t)z+tθ\boldsymbol{\theta}_t = (1-t)\mathbf{z} + t\boldsymbol{\theta}θt​=(1−t)z+tθ

, we verify posterior calibration via SBC. For

children.value: column of the behavioral results CSV
Extracts the trial schedule from the same file (episode, cue_identity, actual_vector_angle, outcome_occurred, is_oddball)
Computes the identical 40 features used during training
Passes the feature vector through the trained network
Returns 4,000 posterior samples per parameter, from which means, standard deviations, and credible intervals are computed*
Clips samples to prior bounds to prevent out-of-support estimates
*Posterior means, standard deviations, and 95% highest density intervals are computed from the clipped samples.

children.label: *Posterior means, standard deviations, and 95% highest density intervals are computed from the clipped samples.
